source.title: Making working memory work: a computational model of learning in the prefrontal cortex and basal ganglia

confidence_rationale: The PBWM model independently motivates a multi-gate BG architecture with functionally distinct input gate (sensorium), maintenance gate (thought loop), and output gate (action commitment), all implemented via the canonical striatum-SNr-thalamus-PFC disinhibition loop. Strong mapping fidelity to Q-019 sensorium and commitment gates; moderate transfer risk as this is a computational model not direct anatomy.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The basal ganglia perform dynamic gating of PFC working memory via dopamine-trained Go/NoGo competition; two gate types operate: input gating (what enters active PFC maintenance) and output gating (which maintained representation drives downstream action selection).
mapping.ree_translation: Input gating = sensorium gate (BG selects which sensory/perceptual representations enter the processing loop); PFC stripe maintenance = thought loop gate (multiple independently-gated WM slots enable E3 complex operations); output gating = action commitment gate (BG releases maintained representation to drive motor/action execution).
mapping.mapping_caveat: PBWM treats all gates as instances of the same canonical circuit form; REE adds distinct oscillatory control planes (beta vs theta) and anatomical specificity. This paper does not address the hyperdirect STN pathway or oscillatory signatures. Transfer risk: computational model, not direct empirical anatomy.
mapping.source_context: Neural Computation, 2006. Foundational PBWM paper establishing BG as working memory gating controller.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (foundational, widely cited). High mapping fidelity for three-gate functional separation. Moderate transfer risk from computational model to biological implementation.
